channel.title: Jacopo Castellano Blog
channel.description: Senior Software Engineer & Video Designer crafting elegant digital experiences at the intersection of code and visual storytelling.

item.title: Claude Opus 5: What Shipped and What Users Reported
item.description: Opus 5 holds Opus 4.8 pricing at $5 in and $25 out and comes close to Fable 5. A summary of the published benchmarks, the new features, and what users reported in the first hours.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of July 13, 2026)
item.description: The week open-weight models caught the frontier labs, the security bill for agentic AI came due, and the buildout hit power grids and memory chips.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of July 6, 2026)
item.description: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.6 and lost its safety chief in the same week, Apple sued it, a price war broke out, and the bill for AI compute started landing on the power grid.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of June 29, 2026)
item.description: The week Washington reversed itself on frontier-model exports, Anthropic shipped Sonnet 5 before its IPO, and governments bet trillions on robots. The 10 biggest AI stories, counting down.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of June 22, 2026)
item.description: The ten biggest AI stories of the week, from Washington gatekeeping frontier models to the great escape from Nvidia and the memory crunch hitting your wallet.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of June 15, 2026)
item.description: The week a US order pulled two frontier models offline, plus the sovereign-AI surge, China's open models, the chip race, and the agent security reckoning.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of June 8, 2026)
item.description: The week AI stopped acting like an industry and started acting like infrastructure: a top model pulled offline, the first trillionaire minted, and Meta's AI unit in revolt.

item.title: Claude Fable 5: A New Model, Free Until June 22 (Update: Pulled Offline by the US Government)
item.description: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 from Anthropic are the same model with different safeguards. Fable 5 is free on paid plans until June 22, then needs usage credits. What changed, and what to do before the window closes.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of June 1, 2026)
item.description: The 10 biggest AI stories of the week, from Anthropic's record IPO filing to a developer revolt over AI pricing and a power grid buckling under data centers.

item.title: AI Weekly Digest: The Top 10 Things to Know This Week (Week of May 25, 2026)
item.description: The 10 biggest AI stories from the week of May 25-31, 2026: Anthropic's $65B raise and Claude Opus 4.8, a papal encyclical on AI, a widening security crisis, and a buildout hitting its limits.

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Leaked Claude Code's Source. Inside It Was a Tamagotchi.
item.description: A source map leak exposed 512,000 lines of Claude Code's TypeScript. Buried inside was a full Tamagotchi companion system with 18 species, rarity tiers, and RPG stats.

item.title: I built a plugin that changes my terminal tabs when Claude is thinking
item.description: When you're running five Claude sessions at once, a grey tab tells you nothing. TabChroma fixes that with color.

item.title: Chrome DevTools Now Has MCP: What This Actually Changes for Your Workflow
item.description: Chrome M144's --autoConnect flag lets AI coding agents tap into your live browser session. What actually changes, what breaks, and when to use it.

item.title: The CLI Is Making a Comeback, and AI Is the Reason
item.description: GUIs were designed for humans. AI agents are not humans. Here's why CLI-first tooling is having a moment, and what that means for how you build.

item.title: Three AI Agents Is My Limit. Here's What That Taught Me.
item.description: I hit a cognitive ceiling managing multiple AI agents at once. Research puts it at three. Here's what that taught me about designing agents you can actually ignore.

item.title: The $82,000 mistake: how to secure your AI API keys before it's too late
item.description: A developer's stolen Gemini API key ran up $82,314 in 48 hours. Here's what changed about AI API key security and how to protect yourself.

item.title: Claude Code Remote Control and the end of the OpenClaw era
item.description: Anthropic cut off Max license access for OpenClaw and shipped Remote Control a week later. I don't think that's a coincidence.

item.title: The Model Is Not the Bottleneck
item.description: Why AI agent architecture matters more than model selection — and how AT&T cut AI costs 90% by rethinking orchestration, not swapping models.

item.title: Vibe coding is eating open source, and the industry is barely paying attention
item.description: AI tools have made contributing code nearly free, but reviewing it costs the same. Here's what that asymmetry is doing to open source maintainers.

item.title: The week AI agents got credentials, and I started locking mine down
item.description: I realized I had no idea what my AI tool integrations were actually allowed to do. Here's the prompt injection risk, and the four changes I made.

item.title: The AI Skills Gap Nobody's Talking About
item.description: Every developer uses AI tools daily. The businesses paying our salaries mostly don't. Here's what that gap means for your career.

item.title: I Built a Personal Assistant to Manage 160+ Project Folders
item.description: When manual tracking stops working, automate visibility, not organization.

item.title: Why I Give My AI Agents Human Names
item.description: A tiny tweak that made my AI tools feel like teammates

item.title: Claude Code 2.0: My First Impressions
item.description: On September 29th, I woke up to find Claude Code 2.0 in my terminal. No fanfare, no countdown, just there, ready to go.

item.title: The Case for Paid Password Managers: Lessons from a DIY Approach
item.description: Exploring the pros & cons of building a DIY password manager with Python, and why professional tools like 1Password are worth the investment

item.title: 3 Steps to an Organized Workspace: Automating File Organization
item.description: The very personal, customizable and automated system that I use to organize all my files

item.title: 5 Advanced Bash Aliases Ready To Be Used
item.description: Powerful shortcuts for your command line: Advanced techniques using parameters and functions to level up your CLI game

item.title: How to Use Bash Aliases to Run Scripts More Efficiently
item.description: To execute commands in the terminal easily and intuitively, without having to remember long, indecipherable strings of words, bash aliases…

item.title: How to keep your AI prompts organized with your own Prompt Manager
item.description: In the AI era, managing prompts efficiently is becoming increasingly challenging. Here is a solution that works for me (free download link…

item.title: DIY Video Conversion: Python Scripts for fast GUI-free Hap conversions
item.description: A simple CLI wrapper for FFmpeg.

item.title: Video Design for Classical Music: Less Is More
item.description: Why restraint is the most important skill when creating visuals for live classical performances.

item.title: From Music to Code: What Piano Taught Me About Programming
item.description: The unexpected parallels between classical piano training and software engineering.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Plugins: Empowering AI with Real-time Information and Third-party Services
item.description: OpenAI takes ChatGPT to the next level by integrating plugins, enabling more capabilities, and enhancing user experience

item.title: Chuck Norris Comes to MS Teams: A Joke Generator Script
item.description: Running out of ideas to boost morale and relieve the stress of your team during the long winter months? Look no further! An automated…

item.title: The Steam-Powered Stream Snatcher
item.description: The Ultimate Tool for Harvesting YouTube Videos with Elegance and Efficiency (In Python)

item.title: Requirements Management: The Dark Magic of Project Success
item.description: Let’s talk about the “whats, hows and whys” of Requirements Management.

item.title: 7 benefits of memorizing the first 2000 digits of π
item.description: And why on earth would you do it in the first place.

item.title: How to Extract Email Addresses from a Text File with Regular Expressions and Bash
item.description: Use regex and `grep` to easily extract email addresses from a text file.

item.title: The Ultimate (Easy) Guide to Setting Up SSH Key Pairs
item.description: Eliminate the need for passwords with SSH key pairs! Get started with this handy guide and take your security to the next level.

item.title: Regular expressions made simple: An introduction to regex in bash
item.description: Regular expressions, also known as regex or regexp, are a powerful tool for manipulating and extracting information from text-based data…

item.title: Automate Your Backups with Bash Scripts
item.description: Say goodbye to manual backups! This collection of scripts makes it easy to automate your backups and protect your valuable data. For free.
